summary.intent: Fix bugs in the ars-ui Progress component on PR #682: preserve controlled value as source of truth in Complete state, sanitize circular radius before SVG dash metrics, and make uncontrolled Reset transition to Loading instead of Idle.
summary.outcome: All three fixes were implemented and passed locally (integration tests, module tests, proptest, fmt, clippy, spec validation). Commit 85d84b2e was pushed, Codex review threads were resolved, and a new review cycle was triggered. The session ends mid-CI-wait for the new run.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot (@codex) for automated PR review; each push must be followed by resolving open threads and posting '@codex review' to restart the review cycle.

CI pipeline has two phases: fast checks (Formatting, Snapshot Change Check, Clippy, Workspace Check, Mutual Exclusion Guard, Unit Tests) then a longer downstream matrix (adapter, browser, coverage, feature-flag, release jobs).
The Progress component spec lives at spec/components/data-display/progress.md and is validated via 'cargo xtask spec validate' — spec must be kept in sync with implementation.
Progress state machine: controlled props are the source of truth; uncontrolled Reset must resolve to Loading (not Idle) when it clears value to indeterminate.
The meter API shape (controlled vs uncontrolled distinction) is an explicit contract that should not be changed within a single PR even when Codex suggests it.

code.finding: Reset event handling: uncontrolled reset must derive state from the effective value (indeterminate → Loading), not unconditionally go to Idle.
Circular dash metrics (circle_stroke_dashoffset, circle_range_attrs) must sanitize invalid/negative radii to 0 before computing SVG dash values to avoid math errors.
Integration test coverage for reset→loading transition and circular radius edge cases was added/updated in this file.
Proptest state machine was updated to disallow reset→idle transitions, enforcing the corrected invariant.
Spec had stale documentation showing reset returning to Idle and unsanitized circular dash math; both were corrected to match the reviewed implementation.

workflow: The polling loop pattern (sleep + gh pr checks + reactions + GraphQL for unresolved threads in one compound command) is the established approach for waiting on CI and Codex review simultaneously.
Commit co-author trailer for Codex contributions uses: 'Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>'
Red-green workflow was followed: failing test expectations were written first, then implementation fixed to make them pass.
After each push: (1) reply to Codex thread with fix details, (2) resolve thread via GraphQL mutation, (3) post '@codex review' comment, (4) enter CI wait loop.

friction: Long CI pipeline with two sequential phases meant multiple polling rounds (each 60–180s sleep) before knowing if the full run was green.
Codex raised a thread about the meter API shape that conflicted with an explicit design contract — required a reasoning reply rather than a code change, adding overhead.
The spec at spec/components/data-display/progress.md had drifted from implementation, requiring a separate sync pass after the code fixes.
open_items: CI run 26413296053 triggered by commit 85d84b2e was still in progress (downstream matrix pending) when the session ended — full green status not yet confirmed.
Codex's next review pass after the '@codex review' comment had not yet completed — new threads may open requiring further iteration.
The meter API shape question raised by Codex was deferred with a reasoning reply rather than resolved architecturally — may resurface in future PRs.
